<p>The <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">omnivoice-demo</code> CLI provides a graphical interface for interacting with the OmniVoice model. It allows users to perform zero-shot voice cloning, voice design, and automated text-to-speech without writing code. The UI is built using the <a href="https://gradio.app/" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">Gradio</a> library and exposes the core capabilities of the <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">OmniVoice.generate()</code> pipeline.</p>

<p>The demo is launched via the <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">omnivoice-demo</code> entry point. It supports several flags to configure the server and model backend.</p>

<p><strong>Device Auto-detection Logic:</strong>
The script uses <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">get_best_device()</code> to prioritize hardware acceleration in the following order: <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">cuda</code> -> <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">mps</code> -> <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">cpu</code> <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:38-44]</a>.</p>

<p>This tab implements the <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">VoiceClonePrompt</code> workflow <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:206-212]</a>.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Reference Audio</strong>: An upload component for the source voice <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:236]</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Reference Text (Optional)</strong>: If provided, this text is used as the transcript for the reference audio. If left blank, the system invokes Whisper ASR (unless <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">--no-asr</code> is set) to transcribe the audio automatically <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:209-212]</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Target Text</strong>: The text to be synthesized in the cloned voice.</li>
</ul>

<p>This tab allows users to "construct" a voice using categorical attributes.</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Attribute Selectors</strong>: Dropdowns for Gender, Age, Pitch, Style, English Accent, and Chinese Dialect <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:58-101]</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Instruction Assembly</strong>: Selecting options from these categories dynamically builds a comma-separated <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">instruct</code> string (e.g., "Female, Young Adult, High Pitch") which is passed to the model <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:214-215]</a>.</li>
</ul>

<ul>
<li><strong>Language Selector</strong>: A dropdown containing over 600 supported languages derived from <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">LANG_NAMES</code> <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:50]</a>. Selecting "Auto" allows the model to infer the language from the input text.</li>
<li><strong>Generation Parameters</strong>: Sliders for <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">num_step</code> (diffusion steps), <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">guidance_scale</code>, <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">denoise</code>, <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">speed</code>, and <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">duration</code> <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:187-204]</a>.</li>
</ul>

<p>The <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">_gen_core</code> function handles the conversion of UI state into model-ready tensors and configurations:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Config Initialization</strong>: It instantiates <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">OmniVoiceGenerationConfig</code> using values from the UI sliders, specifically setting <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">num_step</code>, <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">guidance_scale</code>, and <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">denoise</code> <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:187-193]</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Prompt Preparation</strong>:
<ul>
<li>For <strong>Cloning</strong>: It calls <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">model.create_voice_clone_prompt(ref_audio=ref_audio, ref_text=ref_text)</code> <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:209-212]</a>.</li>
<li>For <strong>Design</strong>: It cleans the instruction string from the UI and adds it to the keyword arguments <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:214-215]</a>.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li><strong>Inference</strong>: It executes <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">model.generate(**kw)</code> where <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">kw</code> contains the text, language, and the prepared prompts/configs <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:218]</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Post-processing</strong>: The output <code class="rounded-sm bg-[#e5e5e5] px-[0.25rem] py-[0.20rem] text-xs font-normal leading-[15px] before:hidden after:hidden dark:bg-[#484848]/30">torch.Tensor</code> is converted to a 16-bit PCM NumPy array for Gradio's audio player <a href="" class="text-neutral-300 hover:text-neutral-200 hover:underline">[omnivoice/cli/demo.py:221-224]</a>.</li>
</ol>

OmniVoice Overview\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb](docs/OmniVoice.ipynb)\n- [docs/community-projects.md](docs/community-projects.md)\n- [docs/tips.md](docs/tips.md)\n- [omnivoice/__init__.py](omnivoice/__init__.py)\n- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)\n- [uv.lock](uv.lock)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nOmniVoice is a state-of-the-art zero-shot multilingual Text-to-Speech (TTS) model supporting more than 600 languages [README.md:17-23](). It is built on a novel diffusion language model architecture that operates over discrete audio tokens, delivering high-quality speech synthesis with superior inference speeds—achieving Real-Time Factors (RTF) as low as 0.025 (40x faster than real-time) [README.md:17-30]().\n\nThe system is designed for versatility, offering three distinct generation modes: **Voice Cloning**, **Voice Design**, and **Auto Voice**. It also supports advanced features like inline non-verbal sounds (e.g., `[laughter]`) and fine-grained pronunciation control via Pinyin or phonemes [README.md:23-30]().\n\n### Key Capabilities\n\n*   **Zero-Shot Multilingual TTS**: Supports over 600 languages, providing the broadest language coverage among current zero-shot models [README.md:23-25]().\n*   **Voice Cloning**: High-fidelity cloning of a target speaker from a short (3–10s) reference audio clip [README.md:26](), [README.md:143]().\n*   **Voice Design**: Generates unique voices based on natural language descriptions (instruct strings) covering gender, age, pitch, style (e.g., whisper), and accents/dialects [README.md:27](), [README.md:151-155]().\n*   **Fast Inference**: Optimized diffusion-style architecture allows for rapid generation on both NVIDIA GPUs and Apple Silicon [README.md:29-30](), [README.md:44-62]().\n\n### Architecture Summary\n\nOmniVoice utilizes a diffusion language model architecture. The core model, represented by the `OmniVoice` class, leverages a Qwen-based LLM backbone to perform iterative unmasking and diffusion over multi-codebook discrete audio tokens [README.md:30](), [omnivoice/__init__.py:22-26]().\n\n#### System Logic Flow\nThe following diagram illustrates how high-level concepts map to specific code entities within the OmniVoice repository.\n\n**Diagram: Logic to Code Entity Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        [\"Voice Cloning\"] -- \"ref_audio\" --\u003e D\n        [\"Voice Design\"] -- \"instruct\" --\u003e D\n        [\"Auto Voice\"] -- \"text only\" --\u003e D\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        D[\"model.generate()\"]\n        E[\"OmniVoice Class\"]\n --\u003e G\n    \n    style E stroke-width:4px\n    style D stroke-width:4px\n```\n**Sources:** [README.md:113-180](), [omnivoice/__init__.py:22-26](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:50-51]()\n\n### Technical Components\n\nThe codebase is organized into several functional areas:\n1.  **Inference Engine**: Managed via the `OmniVoice` class, supporting `from_pretrained()` loading and the `generate()` API source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml](.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml)\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb](docs/OmniVoice.ipynb)\n- [docs/community-projects.md](docs/community-projects.md)\n- [docs/tips.md](docs/tips.md)\n- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)\n- [uv.lock](uv.lock)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page provides technical instructions for installing OmniVoice, setting up the required hardware environment, and utilizing the Python API to perform zero-shot speech synthesis across its three primary operational modes.\n\n## Installation\n\nOmniVoice requires Python 3.10 or higher [pyproject.toml:11-11](). It depends on `torch`, `torchaudio`, and the `transformers` library [pyproject.toml:32-35]().\n\n### Hardware Requirements\n*   **NVIDIA GPU**: Recommended for optimal inference speed (RTF ~0.025). CUDA 12.8 is the preferred toolkit version [pyproject.toml:80-82]().\n*   **Apple Silicon**: Supported via the `mps` device mapping [README.md:127-127]().\n*   **Memory**: Sufficient VRAM to load the model weights (typically 16GB+ for float16 inference).\n\n### Setup Methods\n\n#### Using pip\nIt is recommended to install PyTorch with the specific CUDA index first to ensure GPU acceleration is enabled.\n\n```bash\n# Install PyTorch with CUDA 12.8 support\npip install torch==2.8.0+cu128 torchaudio==2.8.0+cu128 --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128\n\n# Install OmniVoice from PyPI\npip install omnivoice\n```\n[README.md:48-50](), [README.md:68-68]()\n\n#### Using uv\nFor developers using the `uv` package manager, the project includes a configured `pyproject.toml` with tool-specific indices for PyTorch [pyproject.toml:78-84]().\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/k2-fsa/OmniVoice.git\ncd OmniVoice\nuv sync\n```\n[README.md:84-86]()\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:34-90](), [pyproject.toml:1-91]()\n\n---\n\n## Loading the Pretrained Model\n\nThe entry point for interacting with the model is the `OmniVoice` class. The `from_pretrained` method handles weight downloading and device allocation.\n\n### Model Initialization Flow\n\nThe diagram below illustrates how the `OmniVoice` class initializes the underlying architecture and prepares for inference.\n\n**OmniVoice Initialization Logic**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Entrypoint: omnivoice/__init__.py\"\n        Init[\"OmniVoice.from_pretrained()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Core Architecture Entities\"\n        OVClass[\"class OmniVoice\"]\n        OVConfig[\"class OmniVoiceConfig\"]\n        Backbone[\"Qwen2Model Backbone\"]\n    end\n\n    Init --\u003e|1. Load Config| OVConfig\n    Init --\u003e|2. Instantiate| OVClass\n    OVClass --\u003e|3. Initialize| Backbone\n    OVClass --\u003e|4. Map Device| Device[\"CUDA / MPS / CPU\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:118-126](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:50-50]()\n\n### Python Example\n```python\nfrom omnivoice import OmniVoice\nimport torch\n\nmodel = OmniVoice.from_pretrained(\n    \"k2-fsa/OmniVoice\",\n    device_map=\"cuda:0\", # Use \"mps\" for Mac\n    dtype=torch.float16,\n    load_asr=True # Load Whisper for automatic ref_text transcription\n)\n```\n[README.md:122-126](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:50-50]()\n\n---\n\n## Generation Modes\n\nThe `model.generate()` method is the unified interface for all synthesis tasks. It accepts text and optional conditioning signals to determine the voice characteristics.\n\n### 1. Voice Cloning\nThis mode uses a short reference audio clip (3–10 seconds recommended) to extract speaker characteristics [README.md:143-143](). If `ref_text` is not provided, the system utilizes a Whisper ASR fallback to transcribe the reference audio automatically [README.md:135-136]().\n\n```python\naudio = model.generate(\n    text=\"Hello, this is a test of zero-shot voice cloning.\",\n    ref_audio=\"path/to/reference.wav\",\n    ref_text=\"The transcription of the reference audio.\" # Optional\n)\n```\n[README.md:129-133](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:77-81]()\n\n### 2. Voice Design\nVoice design allows for \"prompt-engineering\" a voice using natural language attributes (gender, age, pitch, accent) without requiring an audio sample. Attributes are comma-separated [README.md:155-155]().\n\n```python\naudio = model.generate(\n    text=\"Hello, this is a test of zero-shot voice design.\",\n    instruct=\"female, low pitch, british accent\"\n)\n```\n[README.md:158-161](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:98-101]()\n\n### 3. Auto Voice\nIn this mode, the model generates speech without specific speaker conditioning, selecting a voice based on its internal prior distribution [README.md:171-171]().\n\n```python\naudio = model.generate(text=\"This is a sentence without any voice prompt.\")\n```\n[README.md:174-174](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:118-120]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow: Text to Audio\n\nThe following diagram bridges the high-level API calls to the internal code entities responsible for processing the request.\n\n**Inference Data Flow**\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User as \"Python Script\"\n    participant OV as \"omnivoice.OmniVoice\"\n    participant GenCfg as \"OmniVoiceGenerationConfig\"\n    participant ASR as \"Whisper ASR (Optional)\"\n    participant LLM as \"Qwen2Model Backbone\"\n    participant Diff as \"Diffusion Loop\"\n\n    User-\u003e\u003eOV: .generate(text, ref_audio, instruct)\n    Note over OV: Check if ref_text is missing\n    OV-\u003e\u003eASR: Transcription Fallback (if ref_audio exists)\n    OV-\u003e\u003eGenCfg: Initialize with params (num_step, speed, duration)\n    OV-\u003e\u003eOV: Preprocess Text \u0026 Audio Tokens\n    OV-\u003e\u003eLLM: Forward Pass (Context Embedding)\n    loop Diffusion Steps\n        Diff-\u003e\u003eLLM: Iterative Unmasking (Denoise)\n    end\n    OV-\u003e\u003eUser: Return List[np.ndarray] (24kHz)\n```\n\n### Key Parameters\nThe generation behavior can be tuned using the following arguments in `model.generate()`:\n\n| Parameter | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `num_step` | `int` | Number of diffusion steps (default 32; 16 for faster inference) [README.md:184-184]() |\n| `speed` | `float` | Speed factor (\u003e1.0 is faster) [README.md:185-185]() |\n| `duration` | `float` | Target duration in seconds (overrides speed) [README.md:186-186]() |\n| `guidance_scale` | `float` | Classifier-free guidance scale for quality/diversity trade-off |\n\n**Sources:** [README.md:129-186](), [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb:50-123]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"19:T1127,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Supported Languages\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv](docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv)\n- [docs/languages.md](docs/languages.md)\n- [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py](omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nOmniVoice provides extensive multilingual support, covering **646 languages** trained on a massive dataset of approximately **581,000 hours** of audio [docs/languages.md:3-3](). This section details the language identification system, the mapping between natural language names and internal codes, and how to utilize these languages during inference.\n\n## Language Mapping System\n\nOmniVoice uses a unified mapping system to translate human-readable language names into internal language IDs. These IDs are primarily based on ISO 639-3 codes, though some variations exist for specific dialects [docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv:1-1]().\n\n### Core Mapping Logic\nThe system relies on a pre-computed dictionary to resolve user input. This mapping is defined in `omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py`, which is auto-generated from the master TSV file [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py:20-24]().\n\n*   **`LANG_NAME_TO_ID`**: A dictionary mapping lowercase language names (e.g., \"english\") to internal IDs (e.g., \"en\") [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py:29-30]().\n*   **`LANG_IDS`**: A set containing all supported internal ID codes used for validation during the generation process [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py:20-23]().\n\n### Data Flow: Language Resolution\nThe following diagram illustrates how a user-provided language string is resolved to a system ID during the inference initialization phase.\n\n**Language Identification Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        A[\"User Input: --language 'French'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space: omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py\"\n        B[\"lower() Transformation\"]\n        C{\"LANG_NAME_TO_ID Lookup\"}\n        D[\"System ID: 'fr'\"]\n        E[\"Error: Unsupported Language\"]\n    end\n\n    A --\u003e B\n    B --\u003e C\n    C -- \"Match Found\" --\u003e D\n    C -- \"No Match\" --\u003e E\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py:29-209](), [docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv:1-188]()\n\n## Training Data Distribution\n\nThe model's proficiency in a language is generally correlated with the duration of training data available. The distribution ranges from major global languages with over 100,000 hours to low-resource languages with approximately 10 hours of data.\n\n| Language | OmniVoice ID | ISO 639-3 | Training Duration (h) |\n| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: |\n| English | `en` | `eng` | 206,061.1 |\n| Chinese | `zh` | `cmn` | 111,343.3 |\n| Spanish | `es` | `spa` | 27,559.74 |\n| French | `fr` | `fra` | 23,675.32 |\n| German | `de` | `deu` | 21,927.13 |\n| Japanese | `ja` | `jpn` | 13,853.53 |\n| Standard Arabic | `arb` | `arb` | 1,483.53 |\n| Abkhazian | `ab` | `abk` | 57.27 |\n\nSources: [docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv:5-188](), [docs/languages.md:125-173]()\n\n## Specifying Languages during Inference\n\nWhen using the `OmniVoice.generate()` method or the CLI tools, the language can be specified using either the full name or the internal ID.\n\n### Inference Parameters\nThe language parameter influences the text normalization and phonetic estimation stages of the pipeline.\n\n*   **CLI Usage**: Use the `--language` flag with the `omnivoice-infer` tool.\n*   **Python API**: Pass the `language` argument to `model.generate()`.\n\n**Code-to-System Association**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Input Configuration\"\n        CONFIG[\"OmniVoiceGenerationConfig\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Internal Processing\"\n        GEN[\"model.generate()\"]\n        MAP[\"lang_map.py\"]\n        DUR[\"RuleDurationEstimator\"]\n    end\n\n    CONFIG -- \"language='en'\" --\u003e GEN\n    GEN -- \"Lookup\" --\u003e MAP\n    MAP -- \"ID: 'en'\" --\u003e DUR\n    DUR -- \"Phonetic Weighting\" --\u003e Output[\"Audio Tokens\"]\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/utils/lang_map.py:20-24](), [docs/languages.md:5-9]()\n\n### Handling Low-Resource Languages\nFor the 600+ languages with smaller data footprints (often ~10 hours), the model leverages cross-lingual transfer from high-resource languages. The `RuleDurationEstimator` uses Unicode range mapping to determine the appropriate script-based duration logic even if specific phonetic tables for a rare language are limited.\n\nSources: [docs/languages.md:1-10](), [docs/lang_id_name_map.tsv:2-100]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1a:T18bf,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Core Model Architecture\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/models/__init__.py](omnivoice/models/__init__.py)\n- [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py](omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe OmniVoice model is a diffusion-based language model designed for high-fidelity, multilingual speech synthesis. It leverages a large language model (LLM) backbone to perform iterative refinement (unmasking) on discrete audio tokens. This architecture allows OmniVoice to support diverse tasks such as zero-shot voice cloning, attribute-based voice design, and autonomous speech generation across over 600 languages.\n\n## Model Components\n\nThe architecture consists of three primary pillars: a transformer-based backbone, a multi-codebook discrete audio representation, and a diffusion-based generation mechanism.\n\n### 1. LLM Backbone (Qwen)\nOmniVoice utilizes a **Qwen**-based LLM as its core processing engine [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:158-174](). The model is registered with the HuggingFace Transformers library as `OmniVoice`, extending `PreTrainedModel` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:193-196]().\n\n### 2. Multi-Codebook Audio Representation\nAudio is represented as discrete tokens across multiple layers (codebooks).\n- **Vocab \u0026 Codebooks**: The model typically uses 8 audio codebooks with a vocabulary size of 1025 per codebook [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:163-165]().\n- **Embeddings**: Audio tokens are projected into the LLM's hidden dimension using `audio_embeddings` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:209-212]().\n- **Layer Offsets**: To distinguish between tokens from different codebooks, the model applies `codebook_layer_offsets` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:213-216]().\n\n### 3. Diffusion Language Model\nUnlike traditional autoregressive TTS, OmniVoice treats speech generation as a non-autoregressive diffusion process over discrete tokens.\n- **Masking**: The model is trained to predict original tokens from masked versions.\n- **Iterative Unmasking**: During inference, the model starts with a sequence of mask tokens and iteratively \"fills them in\" over a specified number of steps (e.g., 32 steps) [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:93]().\n\n---\n\n## Code Entity Mapping: Architecture to Implementation\n\nThe following diagram bridges the conceptual \"Natural Language Space\" of model components to the specific \"Code Entity Space\" found in `omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py`.\n\n### System Component Map\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        A[\"Model Configuration\"]\n        B[\"Backbone LLM\"]\n        C[\"Audio Token Logic\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        A --\u003e [\"OmniVoiceConfig\"]\n        B --\u003e [\"OmniVoice.llm\"]\n        C --\u003e [\"audio_embeddings\"]\n        C --\u003e [\"audio_heads\"]\n        C --\u003e [\"codebook_layer_offsets\"]\n    end\n\n    [\"OmniVoiceConfig\"] --\u003e [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:157-183]\n    [\"OmniVoice.llm\"] --\u003e [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:198-207]\n    [\"audio_embeddings\"] --\u003e [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:209-212]\n    [\"audio_heads\"] --\u003e [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:217-220]\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:157-220]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow: From Prompt to Tokens\n\nOmniVoice processes three distinct types of inputs (text, voice prompts, and instructions) and maps them into a unified sequence for the transformer.\n\n### Sequence Assembly Diagram\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Input Processing\"\n        Text[\"Target Text\"]\n        RefA[\"Reference Audio\"]\n        RefT[\"Reference Text\"]\n        Inst[\"Instruct String\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entities\"\n        VCP[\"VoiceClonePrompt\"]\n        GT[\"GenerationTask\"]\n    end\n\n    Text --\u003e GT\n    RefA --\u003e VCP\n    RefT --\u003e VCP\n    VCP --\u003e GT\n    Inst --\u003e GT\n\n    GT -- \"model.forward()\" --\u003e LLM[\"Qwen LLM\"]\n    LLM -- \"logits\" --\u003e Output[\"OmniVoiceModelOutput\"]\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:85-150]()\n\n---\n\n## Key Subsystems\n\nThe model's functionality is partitioned into several specialized subsystems that handle the complexities of speech generation.\n\n### Generation Pipeline\nThe `model.generate()` method is the primary entry point for inference. It handles the iterative diffusion loop, classifier-free guidance (CFG), and long-form audio chunking.\n*For details, see [Generation Pipeline](#2.1).*\n\n### Generation Configuration\nInference behavior is governed by the `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-109](). This includes parameters for the diffusion schedule (`num_step`, `t_shift`), sampling temperatures, and audio processing flags (`denoise`, `preprocess_prompt`).\n*For details, see [Generation Configuration Reference](#2.2).*\n\n### Voice Modes\nThe model operates in three distinct modes:\n1.  **Voice Cloning**: Mimics a reference speaker using `ref_audio` and `ref_text` encapsulated in `VoiceClonePrompt` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:85-88]().\n2.  **Voice Design**: Generates a voice based on descriptive attributes (e.g., \"high pitch, fast speed\").\n3.  **Auto Voice**: Generates speech without specific speaker conditioning.\n*For details, see [Voice Modes](#2.3).*\n\n### Duration and Text Utilities\nAccurate speech timing is critical for the diffusion process. The `RuleDurationEstimator` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:63]() predicts the target audio length based on phonetic weights, while text utilities handle punctuation and chunking for long-form synthesis.\n*For details, see [Duration Estimation and Text Utilities](#2.4).*\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table: Core Model Classes\n\n| Class | Purpose | Source |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `OmniVoiceConfig` | Defines LLM backbone, audio vocab size, and codebook weights. | [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:157-183]() |\n| `OmniVoice` | Main model class; manages LLM, embeddings, and `forward`/`generate`. | [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:193-220]() |\n| `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` | Holds hyperparameters for the diffusion sampling process. | [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-109]() |\n| `VoiceClonePrompt` | Dataclass for reference audio tokens and text during cloning. | [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:85-88]() |\n| `GenerationTask` | Internal structure for batching and slicing inference requests. | [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:113-143]() |\n\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:85-220]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1b:T1e3e,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Generation Pipeline\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py](omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\n- [omnivoice/scripts/jsonl_to_webdataset.py](omnivoice/scripts/jsonl_to_webdataset.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/audio.py](omnivoice/utils/audio.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe `model.generate()` method in OmniVoice is a sophisticated inference pipeline that transforms text into high-quality speech through a multi-stage process involving text preprocessing, duration estimation, iterative unmasking diffusion, and audio post-processing. It supports three primary modes: **Voice Cloning**, **Voice Design**, and **Auto Voice**.\n\n## Pipeline Overview\n\nThe generation flow is managed by the `OmniVoice` class [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:193-216](). The pipeline handles everything from raw input string manipulation to the final neural vocoding of discrete audio tokens.\n\n### Data Flow and Code Entities\nThe following diagram maps the logical stages of generation to the specific classes and functions in the codebase.\n\n**Generation Pipeline Architecture**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Input Preprocessing\"\n        A[\"OmniVoice.generate()\"] --\u003e B[\"_prepare_generation_task()\"]\n        B --\u003e C[\"RuleDurationEstimator\"]\n        B --\u003e D[\"add_punctuation()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Conditioning \u0026 Prompting\"\n        E[\"VoiceClonePrompt\"] --\u003e F[\"Whisper ASR (Fallback)\"]\n        F --\u003e G[\"HiggsAudioV2TokenizerModel\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Core Diffusion Loop\"\n        H[\"_generate_short()\"] --\u003e I[\"Iterative Unmasking Loop\"]\n        I --\u003e J[\"Classifier-Free Guidance\"]\n        J --\u003e K[\"layer_penalty_factor\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Long-Form Strategy\"\n        L[\"_generate_long()\"] --\u003e M[\"chunk_text_punctuation()\"]\n        M --\u003e H\n        H --\u003e N[\"cross_fade_chunks()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Output Post-processing\"\n        N --\u003e O[\"remove_silence()\"]\n        O --\u003e P[\"fade_and_pad_audio()\"]\n    end\n\n    style A stroke-width:2px\n    style H stroke-width:2px\n    style L stroke-width:2px\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1016-1112](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1174-1350](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1352-1405]()\n\n---\n\n## 1. Input Preprocessing\n\nBefore the model begins decoding, the input text and reference audio undergo normalization and preparation.\n\n### Text Normalization and Chunking\n*   **Punctuation**: The pipeline ensures the input text ends with appropriate punctuation (e.g., `.` or `。`) using `add_punctuation()` to improve prosody [omnivoice/utils/text.py:207-219]().\n*   **Long-form Splitting**: If the estimated duration exceeds `audio_chunk_threshold` (default 30s), the text is split into smaller segments using `chunk_text_punctuation()` [omnivoice/utils/text.py:119-204](). This function is abbreviation-aware (e.g., it won't split on \"Mr.\") to maintain semantic integrity [omnivoice/utils/text.py:62-116]().\n\n### Duration Estimation\nThe model requires a target sequence length for the audio tokens.\n*   **Rule-Based Estimation**: `RuleDurationEstimator` calculates the required number of audio frames based on character counts and script-specific weights (e.g., CJK characters vs. Latin characters) [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:34-118]().\n*   **Speed Control**: The `speed` parameter scales this estimate. A speed of 1.2x reduces the target length by dividing the estimate by 1.2 [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1020-1035]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:996-1050](), [omnivoice/utils/text.py:207-219](), [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:34-118]()\n\n---\n\n## 2. Voice Conditioning Modes\n\nThe pipeline adapts based on the provided conditioning signals:\n\n| Mode | Input Requirements | Logic |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Voice Cloning** | `ref_audio`, `ref_text` (optional) | Uses Whisper ASR if `ref_text` is missing. Preprocesses reference audio to remove silence [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:954-994](). |\n| **Voice Design** | `instruct` string | Maps natural language attributes (e.g., \"whispering\") to internal instruction tokens [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1134-1155](). |\n| **Auto Voice** | None | Generates speech using default internal embeddings [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1115-1132](). |\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:910-994]()\n\n---\n\n## 3. The Iterative Unmasking Loop\n\nThe core of OmniVoice is a non-autoregressive diffusion process over discrete audio tokens. This happens within `_generate_short()` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1174-1350]().\n\n### The Diffusion Mechanism\n1.  **Initialization**: The audio sequence is initialized with a special `audio_mask_id` (default 1024) across all 8 codebook layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1210-1215]().\n2.  **Iterative Refinement**: Over `num_step` (default 32), the model predicts the most likely tokens for the masked positions.\n3.  **Confidence-Based Unmasking**: In each step, a portion of the masks is replaced by actual tokens based on the model's prediction confidence [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1320-1345]().\n4.  **Layer Penalty**: The `layer_penalty_factor` (default 5.0) ensures that lower (more significant) codebook layers are unmasked before higher (fine-detail) layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1305-1315]().\n\n### Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG)\nTo improve adherence to the prompt (voice or instruction), the model performs two forward passes per step:\n*   **Conditional**: Prompt tokens are provided.\n*   **Unconditional**: Prompt tokens are masked or dropped.\nThe final logits are extrapolated: $Logits_{final} = Logits_{uncond} + guidance\\_scale \\times (Logits_{cond} - Logits_{uncond})$ [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1240-1270]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1210-1350](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-98]()\n\n---\n\n## 4. Long-Form Generation Strategy\n\nFor texts longer than the threshold, the `_generate_long()` method orchestrates the chunking strategy [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1352-1405]().\n\n**Long-Form Execution Flow**\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant M as OmniVoice.generate()\n    participant L as _generate_long()\n    participant S as _generate_short()\n    participant P as AudioUtilities\n\n    M-\u003e\u003eL: Text \u003e audio_chunk_threshold\n    loop For each Text Chunk\n        L-\u003e\u003eS: Generate Audio Segment\n        S--\u003e\u003eL: Return Waveform\n    end\n    L-\u003e\u003eP: cross_fade_chunks()\n    P--\u003e\u003eM: Final Continuous Audio\n```\n\n*   **Chunking**: Text is split at punctuation boundaries [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1365-1375]().\n*   **Cross-Fading**: To prevent audible \"clicks\" or transitions between segments, chunks are merged using `cross_fade_chunks()`, which applies a linear fade-in/out overlap [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:238-275]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1352-1405](), [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:238-275]()\n\n---\n\n## 5. Output Post-Processing\n\nOnce the tokens are generated and decoded into a waveform via the `HiggsAudioV2TokenizerModel` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1100-1105](), the following steps are applied:\n\n1.  **Silence Removal**: Long silences within the generated audio are trimmed using `remove_silence()`, which leverages `pydub`'s `split_on_silence` [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:150-186]().\n2.  **Fade and Pad**: To ensure a professional finish, `fade_and_pad_audio()` applies a 0.1s smooth fade-in/out and adds a tiny buffer of pure silence to the start and end [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:208-236]().\n3.  **RMS Normalization**: If a reference audio was provided, the output volume is matched to the reference RMS level to ensure consistent loudness [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1100-1110]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:150-186](), [omnivoice/utils/audio.py:208-236](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:1090-1112]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1c:T1ff3,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Generation Configuration Reference\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/generation-parameters.md](docs/generation-parameters.md)\n- [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py](omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page provides a comprehensive technical reference for `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig`, the primary data structure used to control the behavior of the OmniVoice inference pipeline. These parameters influence the iterative unmasking process, classifier-free guidance, sampling randomness, and long-form audio segmentation.\n\n## Configuration Overview\n\nThe generation configuration can be managed in two ways:\n1.  **Direct Keyword Arguments**: Passing parameters directly to `model.generate(...)` [docs/generation-parameters.md:5-7]().\n2.  **Configuration Object**: Instantiating an `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` object and passing it to the `generate` method [docs/generation-parameters.md:9-13]().\n\nThe `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` class is defined as a dataclass in the core model file [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:91-103]().\n\n### Configuration Flow\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how the `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` interacts with the `OmniVoice` model and the `GenerationTask` during the inference lifecycle.\n\n**Figure 1: Generation Configuration Data Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Input_Space\"\n        A[\"User_Parameters\"]\n        B[\"OmniVoiceGenerationConfig\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code_Entity_Space\"\n        C[\"OmniVoice.generate\"]\n        D[\"GenerationTask\"]\n        E[\"OmniVoice._generate_batch\"]\n        F[\"OmniVoice._iterative_unmasking\"]\n    end\n\n    A --\u003e|kwargs| C\n    B --\u003e|generation_config| C\n    C --\u003e|Extract_Params| D\n    D --\u003e|get_indices| E\n    E --\u003e|Apply_num_step_guidance_scale| F\n    B --\u003e|audio_chunk_threshold| D\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:91-103](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:112-128](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:5-14]()\n\n---\n\n## Parameter Reference\n\n### Decoding Parameters\nThese parameters control the fundamental behavior of the Diffusion Language Model (DLM) iterative process.\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `num_step` | `int` | `32` | The number of iterative unmasking steps. Higher values improve quality but increase latency. |\n| `guidance_scale` | `float` | `2.0` | Classifier-free guidance (CFG) scale. Controls how strongly the model follows the conditioning (text/voice). |\n| `t_shift` | `float` | `0.1` | Time-step shift for the noise schedule. Lower values focus more on early decoding steps. |\n| `denoise` | `bool` | `True` | If enabled, prepends the `\u003c|denoise|\u003e` token to the input to signal cleaner speech production. |\n\n**Technical Detail**: The `num_step` parameter determines the granularity of the unmasking schedule. A value of `16` is recommended for high-speed inference, while `32` or `64` are used for maximum quality [docs/generation-parameters.md:20]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-99](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:16-23]()\n\n### Sampling and Penalty Parameters\nThese parameters adjust the stochastic nature of token selection and the relationship between different audio codebook layers.\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `layer_penalty_factor` | `float` | `5.0` | Penalty applied to deeper codebook layers. Higher values force the model to unmask lower (more critical) layers first. |\n| `position_temperature` | `float` | `5.0` | Temperature for selecting which masked positions to fill next. `0.0` is deterministic. |\n| `class_temperature` | `float` | `0.0` | Temperature for sampling specific token values. `0.0` is greedy (deterministic). |\n\n**Layer Penalty Implementation**: In the OmniVoice architecture, there are 8 codebook layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:179](). The `layer_penalty_factor` ensures hierarchical consistency by prioritizing the reconstruction of coarse acoustic features before fine-grained details [docs/generation-parameters.md:31]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:95-98](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:27-31]()\n\n### Duration and Speed Control\nOmniVoice supports both explicit duration targets and relative speed adjustments. These can be passed as single values or per-item lists for batching [docs/generation-parameters.md:35]().\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `duration` | `float` | `None` | Fixed output duration in seconds. Overrides `speed`. |\n| `speed` | `float` | `None` | Speed factor (e.g., `1.2` for 20% faster). Ignored if `duration` is set. |\n\n**Priority Logic**: `duration` \u003e `speed`. If both are `None`, the model defaults to a speed of `1.0` using the `RuleDurationEstimator` [docs/generation-parameters.md:45-50]().\n\nSources: [docs/generation-parameters.md:33-50](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:121-122]()\n\n### Pre/Post Processing Parameters\nThese flags control the automatic cleaning of audio data before and after the core generation loop.\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `preprocess_prompt` | `bool` | `True` | Whether to remove long silences in reference audio and normalize reference text [docs/generation-parameters.md:56](). |\n| `postprocess_output` | `bool` | `True` | Apply post-processing (silence removal) to the final generated waveform [docs/generation-parameters.md:57](). |\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:100-101](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:53-57]()\n\n### Long-Form Generation Parameters\nThese settings manage the automatic chunking of long text inputs to maintain constant VRAM consumption regardless of sequence length [docs/generation-parameters.md:61]().\n\n| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `audio_chunk_duration` | `float` | `15.0` | Target duration for individual chunks when splitting long text. |\n| `audio_chunk_threshold` | `float` | `30.0` | The estimated duration limit (in seconds) that triggers the chunking pipeline. |\n\n**Implementation**: The `GenerationTask.get_indices` method uses `audio_chunk_threshold` multiplied by the `frame_rate` to categorize tasks into `short_idx` and `long_idx` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:124-128]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:102-103](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:59-67]()\n\n---\n\n## System Interaction Diagram\n\nThis diagram maps the high-level configuration parameters to the internal logic used during the unmasking loop.\n\n**Figure 2: Configuration Mapping to Code Entities**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"OmniVoiceGenerationConfig\"\n        GS[\"guidance_scale\"]\n        LPF[\"layer_penalty_factor\"]\n        PT[\"position_temperature\"]\n        TS[\"t_shift\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Inference_Logic\"\n        DLM[\"OmniVoice._iterative_unmasking\"]\n        SCHED[\"Noise_Schedule_Calculation\"]\n        SAMP[\"Token_Sampling_Logic\"]\n    end\n\n    GS --\u003e|Scale_Logits| DLM\n    TS --\u003e|Adjust_Alpha_Bar| SCHED\n    LPF --\u003e|Modify_Mask_Scores| SAMP\n    PT --\u003e|Gumbel_Softmax_Positions| SAMP\n\n    SCHED --\u003e DLM\n    SAMP --\u003e DLM\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-103](), [docs/generation-parameters.md:16-31]()\n\n## Trade-offs and Best Practices\n\n1.  **Latency vs. Quality**: Decreasing `num_step` from 32 to 16 provides a near 2x speedup with a slight reduction in prosodic nuance [docs/generation-parameters.md:20]().\n2.  **Guidance Scale**: A `guidance_scale` of 2.0 is standard. Increasing it to 3.0+ can improve speaker similarity in Voice Cloning but may introduce robotic artifacts or \"over-processed\" audio [docs/generation-parameters.md:22]().\n3.  **Denoising**: The `denoise=True` flag is highly recommended for most tasks as it invokes the `\u003c|denoise|\u003e` special token, which leverages the model's ability to filter out background noise present in the training data [docs/generation-parameters.md:21]().\n4.  **Deterministic vs. Stochastic**: For production environments requiring reproducible outputs, set `position_temperature` and `class_temperature` to `0.0` [docs/generation-parameters.md:29-30]().\n\nSources: [docs/generation-parameters.md:1-67]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1d:T1985,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Voice Modes\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [docs/voice-design.md](docs/voice-design.md)\n- [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py](omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py](omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nOmniVoice provides three distinct generation modes to control the identity and characteristics of the synthesized speech. These modes allow users to either replicate an existing voice, design a new one using natural language descriptors, or let the model autonomously select a speaker. All modes are unified under the `model.generate()` interface [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:21-26]().\n\n## 1. Voice Cloning\n\nVoice Cloning (Zero-Shot TTS) allows the model to replicate a speaker's identity based on a short reference audio clip (typically 3-10 seconds).\n\n### Technical Implementation\nThe cloning process relies on the `VoiceClonePrompt` dataclass [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:84-89](). When `ref_audio` is provided to `model.generate()`, the following data flow occurs:\n\n1.  **Audio Tokenization**: The reference audio is processed by the `HiggsAudioV2TokenizerModel` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:49]() to extract discrete audio tokens across 8 codebook layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:165-182]().\n2.  **Transcription (Whisper Fallback)**:\n    *   If `ref_text` is provided, it is used directly as the prompt prefix.\n    *   If `ref_text` is omitted, the model automatically invokes **Whisper ASR** to transcribe the reference audio [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:59-65]().\n3.  **Conditioning**: The extracted `ref_audio_tokens` and the corresponding `ref_text` are encapsulated into a `GenerationTask` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:113-122](). The model uses these as a prefix to maintain speaker consistency and prosody during the iterative unmasking process.\n\n### Data Structure: VoiceClonePrompt\n| Field | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `ref_audio_tokens` | `torch.Tensor` | Shape (8, T), discrete tokens from the codec. |\n| `ref_text` | `str` | Normalized transcription of the reference audio. |\n| `ref_rms` | `float` | Root Mean Square value used for volume normalization. |\n\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:84-122](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:177-182]()\n\n---\n\n## 2. Voice Design\n\nVoice Design enables the creation of synthetic voices using an `instruct` string. This mode maps natural language attributes to the model's latent speaker space without requiring reference audio.\n\n### Attribute Categories and Normalization\nAttributes are categorized into mutually exclusive sets within `_INSTRUCT_CATEGORIES` [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:31-46](). The model supports bilingual instructions (English and Chinese) and performs internal normalization.\n\n| Category | Supported Attributes (Examples) |\n| :--- | :--- |\n| **Gender** | `male`, `female`, `男`, `女` |\n| **Age** | `child`, `young adult`, `elderly`, `少年`, `中年` |\n| **Pitch** | `low pitch`, `high pitch`, `低音调`, `极高音调` |\n| **Style** | `whisper`, `耳语` |\n| **Accent (EN)** | `american accent`, `british accent`, `indian accent` |\n| **Dialect (ZH)** | `四川话`, `东北话`, `河南话`, `陕西话` |\n\n### Implementation Logic\nThe `omnivoice.utils.voice_design` module handles the mapping and validation of these strings:\n*   **`_INSTRUCT_MUTUALLY_EXCLUSIVE`**: A list of sets ensuring that conflicting attributes (e.g., \"male\" and \"female\") are not processed simultaneously [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:50-57]().\n*   **`_INSTRUCT_EN_TO_ZH` / `_INSTRUCT_ZH_TO_EN`**: Translation dictionaries that allow mixed-language strings like `\"female, 青年\"` to be normalized to a consistent internal representation [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:48-54]().\n*   **Bilingual Handling**: The regex `_ZH_RE` identifies Chinese characters to route attributes to `_INSTRUCT_VALID_ZH` or `_INSTRUCT_VALID_EN` [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:27-66]().\n\n**Sources:** [docs/voice-design.md:34-104](), [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:27-67]()\n\n---\n\n## 3. Auto Voice\n\nAuto Voice is the default mode when neither `ref_audio` nor `instruct` is provided.\n\n*   **Behavior**: The model samples a speaker identity from its learned distribution.\n*   **Use Case**: Ideal for generic narrations where a specific voice identity is not required.\n*   **Code Trigger**: In `model.generate()`, the `GenerationTask` is initialized with `ref_audio_tokens=None` and `instructs=None`. The model then proceeds with unconditioned generation [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:113-122]().\n\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:113-143]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow and Code Entity Mapping\n\nThe following diagrams illustrate how user inputs translate into internal code structures and the logic flow within the generation pipeline.\n\n### Diagram: Input Space to Code Entity Mapping\nThis diagram shows how the three modes are parsed into the `GenerationTask` object.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"User_Input_Space\"\n        A1[\"ref_audio + ref_text\"] -- \"Voice Cloning\" --\u003e B\n        A2[\"instruct string\"] -- \"Voice Design\" --\u003e B\n        A3[\"text only\"] -- \"Auto Voice\" --\u003e B\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code_Entity_Space_(omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\"\n        B[\"model.generate()\"]\n        C[\"GenerationTask\"]\n        D[\"VoiceClonePrompt\"]\n        \n        B --\u003e C\n        C --\u003e|ref_audio_tokens| D\n        C --\u003e|instructs| E[\"List[Optional[str]]\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Utility_Space\"\n        F[\"Whisper_ASR_Fallback\"]\n        G[\"_INSTRUCT_ALL_VALID\"]\n    end\n\n    A1 -.-\u003e|if_ref_text_is_None| F\n    F -.-\u003e|transcribes| D\n    A2 -.-\u003e|validation| G\n    G -.-\u003e E\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:84-143](), [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:59-67]()\n\n### Diagram: Voice Design Normalization Flow\nThis diagram details the internal processing of the `instruct` parameter.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Input_Normalization\"\n        IN[\"'female, 青年, british accent'\"] --\u003e SPLIT[\"re.split\"]\n        SPLIT --\u003e CLEAN[\"strip_and_lower\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Logic_(omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py)\"\n        CLEAN --\u003e TRANS[\"_INSTRUCT_ZH_TO_EN\"]\n        TRANS --\u003e VALID[\"_INSTRUCT_ALL_VALID_Check\"]\n        VALID --\u003e EXCL[\"_INSTRUCT_MUTUALLY_EXCLUSIVE_Check\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Model_Injection\"\n        EXCL --\u003e TOK[\"Tokenized_Instruct\"]\n        TOK --\u003e LLM[\"Qwen_LLM_Backbone\"]\n    end\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:25-67](), [docs/voice-design.md:105-120]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1e:T19a7,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Duration Estimation and Text Utilities\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/utils/common.py](omnivoice/utils/common.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/duration.py](omnivoice/utils/duration.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/text.py](omnivoice/utils/text.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe generation pipeline in OmniVoice relies on sophisticated text processing to ensure high-quality audio synthesis. This involves two primary stages: preparing the text for the model via chunking and normalization, and estimating the required audio duration based on the linguistic properties of the input text across more than 600 supported languages.\n\n## Duration Estimation with RuleDurationEstimator\n\nOmniVoice uses a rule-based approach to estimate the duration of generated audio when a specific duration is not provided by the user. The `RuleDurationEstimator` class calculates these estimates by mapping Unicode characters to phonetic weights that represent relative speaking time [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:20-23,31]().\n\n### Phonetic Weight Table\nThe estimator uses a baseline where one Latin character represents a weight of `1.0` (approximately 40-50ms of audio) [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:38-39,55](). Other scripts are weighted relative to this baseline based on their information density and syllabic structure:\n\n| Category | Weight | Script Examples |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `cjk` | 3.0 | Chinese, Japanese Kanji [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:41]() |\n| `hangul` | 2.5 | Korean Hangul [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:43]() |\n| `kana` | 2.2 | Japanese Hiragana/Katakana [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:44]() |\n| `indic` | 1.8 | Hindi, Bengali, Tamil, etc. [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:48]() |\n| `arabic` | 1.5 | Arabic, Persian, Urdu [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:52]() |\n| `digit` | 3.5 | Numbers (expanded during speech) [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:63]() |\n| `punctuation`| 0.5 | Pause capability [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:61]() |\n| `mark` | 0.0 | Diacritics/Accents (Silent) [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:64]() |\n\n### Implementation Details\nThe estimation process utilizes a list of Unicode ranges (`self.ranges`) stored as tuples of `(End_Codepoint, Type_Key)` [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:73](). To ensure high performance, the estimator uses `bisect` for fast binary searching of these ranges [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:25,72]().\n\n**Logic Flow of RuleDurationEstimator**\nThe following diagram illustrates how a string is converted into a duration estimate.\n\nTitle: \"RuleDurationEstimator Logic Flow\"\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"RuleDurationEstimator_Logic\"\n        Input[\"Input_Text_String\"] --\u003e Loop[\"Iterate_Characters\"]\n        Loop --\u003e Unicode[\"Get_ord_char\"]\n        Unicode --\u003e Bisect[\"bisect_left_self_ranges\"]\n        Bisect --\u003e Map[\"Map_to_Script_Category\"]\n        Map --\u003e Weight[\"Lookup_weight_in_self_weights\"]\n        Weight --\u003e Accumulate[\"Sum_total_weight\"]\n        Accumulate --\u003e Final[\"Multiply_by_0_05s_Baseline\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:**\n* [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:31-158]()\n* [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:20-25]()\n\n---\n\n## Text Processing Utilities\n\nText utilities in `omnivoice/utils/text.py` handle the structural preparation of text before it is passed to the diffusion model. This ensures that the model receives text in manageable chunks and with proper terminal punctuation.\n\n### Sentence Splitting and Chunking\nThe function `chunk_text_punctuation` splits long input text into smaller segments based on punctuation boundaries [omnivoice/utils/text.py:119-123]().\n\n*   **Abbreviation Awareness:** The splitter avoids breaking sentences at common abbreviations (e.g., \"Mr.\", \"Dr.\", \"Jan.\") by checking the `ABBREVIATIONS` set [omnivoice/utils/text.py:62-116,152-158]().\n*   **Punctuation Handling:** It identifies split points using `SPLIT_PUNCTUATION` (`.,;:!?。，；：！？`) [omnivoice/utils/text.py:29]().\n*   **Chunk Merging:** After splitting into sentences, it merges short sentences into chunks up to a specified `chunk_len` to optimize model throughput [omnivoice/utils/text.py:166-178]().\n*   **Undersized Chunk Post-processing:** If a `min_chunk_len` is provided, the function merges undersized trailing chunks with previous ones to prevent the model from generating audio for fragments that are too short [omnivoice/utils/text.py:182-198]().\n\n### Punctuation Normalization\nThe `add_punctuation` utility ensures that every input string ends with a valid terminal mark [omnivoice/utils/text.py:207](). This is critical for the model to correctly predict the prosody of the end of an utterance.\n\n*   **Language Detection:** It checks if the text contains Chinese characters using the Unicode range `\\u4e00` to `\\u9fff` [omnivoice/utils/text.py:215]().\n*   **Terminal Marks:** If no terminal punctuation from `END_PUNCTUATION` is present, it appends `。` for Chinese text or `.` for other languages [omnivoice/utils/text.py:214-217,32-59]().\n\n**Sources:**\n* [omnivoice/utils/text.py:29-59]()\n* [omnivoice/utils/text.py:119-204]()\n* [omnivoice/utils/text.py:207-219]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration in Generation Pipeline\n\nThe text utilities and duration estimator are integrated directly into the `OmniVoice.generate()` workflow. This ensures that raw user input is transformed into a format the diffusion model can process effectively.\n\nTitle: \"Text and Duration Utility Integration\"\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    UserText[\"User_Input_Text\"] --\u003e AddPunc[\"add_punctuation\"]\n    AddPunc --\u003e Chunking[\"chunk_text_punctuation\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Per_Chunk_Processing\"\n        Chunking --\u003e Estimator[\"RuleDurationEstimator\"]\n        Estimator --\u003e Dur[\"Calculated_Duration\"]\n        Dur --\u003e Model[\"OmniVoice_Diffusion_Model\"]\n        Chunking --\u003e Model\n    end\n    \n    Model --\u003e Audio[\"Synthesized_Audio\"]\n```\n\n### System Entity Mapping\n\n| System Concept | Code Entity | File Location |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| Text Chunking | `chunk_text_punctuation` | [omnivoice/utils/text.py:119]() |\n| Terminal Punctuation | `add_punctuation` | [omnivoice/utils/text.py:207]() |\n| Duration Logic | `RuleDurationEstimator` | [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:31]() |\n| Abbreviation List | `ABBREVIATIONS` | [omnivoice/utils/text.py:62]() |\n| Script Ranges | `self.ranges` | [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:73]() |\n| Boolean Parsing | `str2bool` | [omnivoice/utils/common.py:27]() |\n| Determinism | `fix_random_seed` | [omnivoice/utils/common.py:46]() |\n\n**Sources:**\n* [omnivoice/utils/text.py:1-220]()\n* [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:1-158]()\n* [omnivoice/utils/common.py:18-57]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1f:T121c,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Command-Line Interfaces\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/cli/__init__.py](omnivoice/cli/__init__.py)\n- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)\n- [uv.lock](uv.lock)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nOmniVoice provides a suite of command-line interfaces (CLIs) designed to facilitate everything from quick local testing to large-scale batch processing and interactive web demonstrations. These tools wrap the underlying `OmniVoice` model class and its `generate` method, providing a user-friendly way to access the model's three primary voice modes: Voice Cloning, Voice Design, and Auto Voice.\n\nThe CLI entrypoints are registered as console scripts in the package configuration, allowing them to be invoked directly from the terminal after installation.\n\n### High-Level CLI Architecture\n\nThe CLI tools act as orchestration wrappers. They handle device detection, model loading via `OmniVoice.from_pretrained`, and argument parsing before passing parameters to the generation pipeline.\n\n**CLI to Code Entity Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"CLI Entrypoints (Natural Language Space)\"\n        A[\"omnivoice-infer\"] \n        B[\"omnivoice-infer-batch\"]\n        C[\"omnivoice-demo\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space (omnivoice/cli/)\"\n        A1[\"omnivoice.cli.infer:main\"]\n        B1[\"omnivoice.cli.infer_batch:main\"]\n        C1[\"omnivoice.cli.demo:main\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Core Model Logic\"\n        D[\"OmniVoice.from_pretrained()\"]\n        E[\"OmniVoice.generate()\"]\n    end\n\n    A --\u003e A1\n    B --\u003e B1\n    C --\u003e C1\n    \n    A1 --\u003e D\n    A1 --\u003e E\n    B1 --\u003e D\n    B1 --\u003e E\n    C1 --\u003e D\n    C1 --\u003e E\n```\n**Sources:** [pyproject.toml:57-60](), [omnivoice/cli/__init__.py:1-1]()\n\n---\n\n### 3.1 omnivoice-infer (Single Inference)\nThe `omnivoice-infer` tool is the primary interface for generating individual speech samples. It supports all model features, including zero-shot cloning from a reference file and attribute-based voice design via natural language instructions.\n\n**Key Capabilities:**\n*   **Voice Cloning**: Pass `--ref_audio` and optionally `--ref_text`. If `--ref_text` is omitted, the tool typically triggers Whisper ASR for transcription.\n*   **Voice Design**: Use the `--instruct` flag to provide attributes (e.g., `\"female, low pitch\"`).\n*   **Hardware Selection**: Automatically detects CUDA or MPS devices.\n\nFor a full reference of all flags (such as `--num_step`, `--guidance_scale`, and `--speed`) and usage examples, see **[omnivoice-infer (Single Inference)](#3.1)**.\n\n**Sources:** [pyproject.toml:58-58]()\n\n---\n\n### 3.2 omnivoice-infer-batch (Batch Inference)\nFor processing large datasets or running evaluations, `omnivoice-infer-batch` provides a high-throughput execution engine. It is designed to consume a manifest file (JSONL) containing multiple generation tasks.\n\n**Key Capabilities:**\n*   **JSONL Manifests**: Reads a list of tasks where each line specifies the text, language, and voice conditioning.\n*   **Parallelism**: Designed to utilize multi-GPU distribution strategies to maximize hardware utilization.\n*   **Structured Output**: Automatically organizes generated `.wav` files into a specified output directory.\n\nFor details on the JSONL schema and multi-GPU configuration, see **[omnivoice-infer-batch (Batch Inference)](#3.2)**.\n\n**Sources:** [pyproject.toml:59-59]()\n\n---\n\n### 3.3 omnivoice-demo (Gradio Web UI)\nThe `omnivoice-demo` command launches a local Gradio-based web interface. This is the most accessible way to experiment with the model's capabilities without managing complex CLI flags.\n\n**Key Capabilities:**\n*   **Interactive Tabs**: Separate interfaces for Voice Cloning and Voice Design.\n*   **Real-time Tuning**: Sliders for parameters like `num_step`, `guidance_scale`, and `speed`.\n*   **Network Configuration**: Supports `--ip` and `--port` flags for hosting the demo.\n\nFor instructions on launching and navigating the UI, see **[omnivoice-demo (Gradio Web UI)](#3.3)**.\n\n**Sources:** [pyproject.toml:60-60](), [pyproject.toml:38-38]()\n\n---\n\n### CLI Implementation Map\n\nThe following table maps the terminal commands to their respective implementation entrypoints within the `omnivoice` source tree.\n\n| Command | Python Entrypoint | Primary Purpose |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `omnivoice-infer` | `omnivoice.cli.infer:main` | Single file generation \u0026 quick testing |\n| `omnivoice-infer-batch` | `omnivoice.cli.infer_batch:main` | Large-scale synthesis \u0026 evaluation |\n| `omnivoice-demo` | `omnivoice.cli.demo:main` | Interactive Gradio Web UI |\n\n**Sources:** [pyproject.toml:57-60]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"20:T1c49,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# omnivoice-infer (Single Inference)\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml](.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml)\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py](omnivoice/cli/infer.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe `omnivoice-infer` CLI is the primary entry point for performing single-item text-to-speech synthesis with OmniVoice. It wraps the high-level `OmniVoice` class API into a command-line tool, supporting all three primary generation modes: **Voice Cloning**, **Voice Design**, and **Auto Voice**.\n\n## Device Auto-Detection Logic\n\nWhen the `--device` flag is not explicitly provided, the CLI employs an automatic detection strategy to select the most performant available hardware.\n\n### Implementation: get_best_device()\nThe detection follows a strict hierarchy defined in the `get_best_device` function [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:33-39]():\n1.  **CUDA**: If `torch.cuda.is_available()` returns `True`, the model defaults to the primary NVIDIA GPU [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:35-36]().\n2.  **MPS**: If running on Apple Silicon and `torch.backends.mps.is_available()` is `True`, it uses the Metal Performance Shaders backend [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:37-38]().\n3.  **CPU**: Falls back to CPU if no specialized accelerators are detected [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:39]().\n\nThe model is loaded with `dtype=torch.float16` by default to optimize VRAM usage and inference speed [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:130-132]().\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:33-39]() (Device detection logic)\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:130-132]() (Model loading implementation)\n\n## CLI Reference\n\nThe CLI uses `argparse` to manage a wide array of parameters ranging from model paths to diffusion sampling hyperparameters [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:42-119]().\n\n### Core Arguments\n| Flag | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `--model` | `str` | `k2-fsa/OmniVoice` | HuggingFace repo ID or local path to checkpoint [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:47-52](). |\n| `--text` | `str` | **Required** | The input text to be synthesized [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:53-58](). |\n| `--output` | `str` | **Required** | Path to save the generated `.wav` file [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:59-64](). |\n| `--language` | `str` | `None` | Language name (e.g., \"English\") or code (e.g., \"en\") [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:86-90](). |\n\n### Mode-Specific Arguments\n| Flag | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `--ref_audio` | `str` | Path to reference audio for **Voice Cloning** [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:66-71](). |\n| `--ref_text` | `str` | Transcript of the reference audio. If omitted, the pipeline may use fallback transcription [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:72-77](). |\n| `--instruct` | `str` | Comma-separated attributes (e.g., \"male, British accent\") for **Voice Design** [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:79-84](). |\n\n### Generation Hyperparameters\n| Flag | Type | Default | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `--num_step` | `int` | `32` | Number of diffusion sampling steps [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:92](). |\n| `--guidance_scale` | `float` | `2.0` | Classifier-free guidance scale [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:93](). |\n| `--speed` | `float` | `1.0` | Speed factor (\u003e1.0 is faster) [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:94](). |\n| `--duration` | `float` | `None` | Fixed output duration in seconds. Overrides speed [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:95-102](). |\n| `--t_shift` | `float` | `0.1` | Time shift for the diffusion scheduler [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:103](). |\n| `--denoise` | `bool` | `True` | Whether to apply the denoiser to the output [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:104](). |\n| `--layer_penalty_factor`| `float` | `5.0` | Penalty factor for layer-wise sampling [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:110](). |\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:42-119]() (Full ArgumentParser definition)\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow and Implementation\n\nThe CLI serves as a bridge between the user's shell and the `OmniVoice.generate()` method. The following diagram illustrates how CLI arguments are mapped to internal model components.\n\n### Data Flow: CLI to Model Engine\nTitle: omnivoice-infer Execution Pipeline\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph CLI_Layer [\"omnivoice.cli.infer\"]\n        A[\"get_parser()\"] --\u003e B[\"main()\"]\n        B --\u003e C[\"get_best_device()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph Model_Layer [\"omnivoice.models.omnivoice\"]\n        C --\u003e D[\"OmniVoice.from_pretrained()\"]\n        B --\u003e E[\"OmniVoice.generate()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph Generation_Pipeline [\"omnivoice.models.omnivoice.generate\"]\n        E --\u003e F[\"Duration Estimation\"]\n        E --\u003e G[\"Diffusion Sampling Loop\"]\n        E --\u003e H[\"Vocoder / Denoise\"]\n    end\n\n    F --\u003e G\n    G --\u003e H\n    H --\u003e I[\"soundfile.write()\"]\n```\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:122-154]() (Main execution flow)\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:33-39]() (Device detection)\n\n---\n\n## Usage Examples\n\n### 1. Voice Cloning\nSynthesize speech using a reference audio file to capture a specific speaker's identity [omnivoice/cli/infer.py:7-10]().\n```bash\nomnivoice-infer \\\n    --model k2-fsa/OmniVoice \\\n    --text \"Zero-shot voice cloning allows me to speak like you.\" \\\n    --ref_audio ./path/to/speaker_ref.wav \\\n    --ref_text \"The transcription of the reference audio.\" \\\n    --output cloned_voice.wav\n```\n\n### 2. (Batch Inference)\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py](omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py](omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe `omnivoice-infer-batch` CLI is designed for high-throughput speech generation across multiple GPUs. It processes a manifest of requests from a JSONL file, distributes them across available hardware using a process pool, and saves the resulting audio files to a structured output directory.\n\n## Overview and Purpose\n\nWhile `omnivoice-infer` is intended for single-sample testing, `omnivoice-infer-batch` provides the necessary infrastructure for large-scale evaluation and production-grade synthesis [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:18-21](). It leverages `ProcessPoolExecutor` to manage worker processes, where each worker holds its own instance of the `OmniVoice` model on a dedicated GPU [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:41-47](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:207-213]().\n\n### Key Capabilities\n*   **Multi-GPU Distribution**: Automatically detects available CUDA or MPS devices and scales workers accordingly [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:56-62]().\n*   **Flexible Generation Modes**: Supports Voice Cloning, Voice Design, and Auto Voice within the same batch file [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:27-32]().\n*   **Throughput Optimization**: Supports both fixed-size batching (`--batch_size`) and duration-based batching (`--batch_duration`), grouping samples by total audio length to maximize GPU utilization [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:141-152]().\n*   **Error Resilience**: Individual task failures are caught within a `try-except` block, logged with tracebacks, and reported without crashing the entire batch process [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:280-285]().\n\nSources: [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:18-62](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:141-152](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:207-213](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:280-285]()\n\n---\n\n## JSONL Test List Schema\n\nThe input to the CLI is a JSONL (JSON Lines) file where each line is a self-contained generation request. The file is parsed by `read_test_list()` [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:29-68]().\n\n### Schema Definition\n\n| Field | Type | Required | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `id` | String | Yes | Unique identifier for the sample (used for the output filename). |\n| `text` | String | Yes | The target text to be synthesized. |\n| `ref_audio` | String | Optional | Path to reference audio (Required for Voice Cloning). |\n| `ref_text` | String | Optional | Transcript of reference audio (If omitted, Whisper ASR is used). |\n| `instruct` | String | Optional | Speaker attributes (e.g., \"male, low pitch\") for Voice Design. |\n| `language_id` | String | Optional | ISO language code (e.g., \"en\", \"zh\"). |\n| `language_name`| String | Optional | Full language name. Used by evaluation scripts, not the model [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:37-40](). |\n| `duration` | Float | Optional | Fixed output duration in seconds. |\n| `speed` | Float | Optional | Speed factor (default: 1.0). |\n\nSources: [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:79-92](), [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:29-68]()\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture and Data Flow\n\nThe batch inference system uses a producer-consumer model. The main process reads the test list and submits tasks to a `ProcessPoolExecutor`.\n\n### Logic Flow Diagram\nThis diagram illustrates the transition from the CLI entry point to the parallel worker processes.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Main Process [omnivoice.cli.infer_batch]\"\n        START[\"CLI Start\"] --\u003e PARSE[\"argparse: get_parser()\"]\n        PARSE --\u003e READ[\"read_test_list()\"]\n        READ --\u003e DETECT[\"get_best_device()\"]\n        DETECT --\u003e QUEUE[\"Create Rank Queue [mp.Queue]\"]\n        QUEUE --\u003e POOL[\"ProcessPoolExecutor\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Worker Processes (N per GPU)\"\n        POOL --\u003e INIT[\"process_init()\"]\n        INIT --\u003e LOAD[\"OmniVoice.from_pretrained()\"]\n        LOAD --\u003e WARM[\"Warmup Inference\"]\n        WARM --\u003e EXEC[\"worker_task()\"]\n        EXEC --\u003e GEN[\"worker_model.generate()\"]\n        GEN --\u003e SAVE[\"sf.write()\"]\n    end\n\n    SAVE --\u003e|\"Return Result\"| POOL\n    POOL --\u003e|\"tqdm Update\"| FINISH[\"All Tasks Complete\"]\n```\n\nSources: [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:56-62](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:207-221](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:254-290](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:317-353]()\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Multi-GPU Distribution Strategy\nThe CLI uses a `multiprocessing.Queue` to distribute GPU ranks to workers. If multiple GPUs are detected, the system spawns `nj_per_gpu` workers for each device [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:119-123](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:321-332]().\n\n1.  **Device Detection**: `get_best_device()` checks for CUDA, then MPS, then CPU [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:56-62]().\n2.  **Rank Assignment**: A list of tuples `(device_type, device_id)` is populated and put into a `rank_queue` [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:321-330]().\n3.  **Initialization**: `process_init()` pulls a rank from the queue and initializes the global `worker_model` for that specific process [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:207-221]().\n\n### Worker Execution Logic\nThe `worker_task` function is the core execution unit for each sample in the JSONL [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:254-290]().\n\n*   **Dynamic Configuration**: It extracts `duration`, `speed`, and `language` overrides from the JSON object [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:261-267]().\n*   **Model Invocation**: Calls `worker_model.generate()` with the parameters provided in the CLI and the specific JSONL line [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:269-278]().\n*   **Output Management**: Saves the generated tensor to `{res_dir}/{id}.wav` using `soundfile.write` at 24kHz [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:66](), [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:286-288]().\n\n### Code Entity Mapping\nThe following diagram maps high-level CLI concepts to specific classes and functions in the codebase.\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class CLI_Entry {\n        +main()\n        +get_parser()\n    }\n    class DataHandling {\n        +read_test_list(path)\n        +load_audio(path)\n    }\n    class WorkerManagement {\n        +process_init(rank_queue, model_checkpoint)\n        +worker_task(sample, args)\n    }\n    class ModelCore {\n        +OmniVoice.from_pretrained()\n        +model.generate()\n    }\n\n    CLI_Entry --\u003e DataHandling : \"Uses omnivoice.utils.data_utils\"\n    CLI_Entry --\u003e WorkerManagement : \"Spawns via ProcessPoolExecutor\"\n    WorkerManagement --\u003e ModelCore : \"Uses omnivoice.models.omnivoice\"\n```\n\nSources: [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:47-53](), custom_id_voice_clone.wav\n└── ...\n```\n\nThe system ensures that the directory exists before starting workers by calling `os.makedirs(args.res_dir, exist_ok=True)`
