files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/hubert.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/hubert.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/minimax.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/minimax.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/norm_config_module.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/norm_config_module.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/punctuations.lst](omnivoice/eval/wer/punctuations.lst)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/wer/text_norm_omni.py](omnivoice/eval/wer/text_norm_omni.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe OmniVoice evaluation framework provides a comprehensive subsystem for calculating Word Error Rate (WER) and Character Error Rate (CER) across diverse datasets and languages. Because OmniVoice is a multilingual model, the evaluation suite employs specialized ASR backends tailored to specific test sets and scripts to ensure high-fidelity transcription for comparison against ground truth.\n\n## Subsystem Architecture\n\nThe evaluation system is structured into dataset-specific drivers that utilize various ASR models (Whisper, HuBERT, Paraformer, SenseVoice, and Omnilingual-ASR). These drivers share common utilities for metric calculation and text normalization.\n\n### ASR Backends by Dataset\n| Dataset | ASR Backend Model | Metrics | Target Languages |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **LibriSpeech-PC** | HuBERT-Large (LS960-ft) | WER | English |\n| **Seed-TTS / MiniMax** | Whisper-large-v3 \u0026 Paraformer | WER | English, Chinese |\n| **Cantonese (yue)** | SenseVoiceSmall | CER | Cantonese |\n| **FLEURS** | Omnilingual-ASR (7B) | WER | 100+ Languages |\n\n### Evaluation Data Flow\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how generated audio is processed through the evaluation pipeline to produce error rates.\n\n**Evaluation Data Flow: From Audio to Metrics**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"InputSpace\"\n        A[\"GeneratedWavs\"] --\u003e B[\"test.jsonl\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Execution:ProcessPoolExecutor\"\n        B --\u003e C[\"run_eval_worker\"]\n        C --\u003e D[\"ASRInferencePipeline\"]\n        D --\u003e E[\"RawHypothesisText\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"NormalizationSpace\"\n        E --\u003e F[\"post_process\"]\n        G[\"GroundTruthText\"] --\u003e F\n        F --\u003e H[\"text_normalize\"]\n        H --\u003e I[\"NormalizedPairs\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"MetricSpace\"\n        I --\u003e J[\"process_one\"]\n        J --\u003e K[\"jiwer.compute_measures\"]\n        K --\u003e L[\"log_metrics\"]\n    end\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py:27-65](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:215-270](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py:241-310]()\n\n---\n\n## Text Normalization and Configuration\n\nTo ensure fair comparison, both the ASR hypothesis and the ground truth transcript undergo rigorous normalization. This is handled primarily by `text_normalize` in `text_norm_omni.py`, which relies on language-specific configurations defined in `norm_config_module.py`.\n\n### Key Normalization Components\n*   **`text_normalize`**: The central function that applies Unicode normalization (NFKC), case folding, punctuation removal, and digit filtering based on the provided ISO language code [omnivoice/eval/wer/text_norm_omni.py:23-113]().\n*   **`norm_config`**: A dictionary mapping ISO codes to specific normalization rules (e.g., whether to remove diacritics or how to handle specific digit sets) [omnivoice/eval/wer/norm_config_module.py:252-280]().\n*   **`punctuations.lst`**: A comprehensive list of Unicode punctuation characters across multiple scripts (Tibetan, Ethiopic, Arabic, etc.) used to build regex patterns for text cleaning [omnivoice/eval/wer/punctuations.lst:1-188](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/norm_config_module.py:187-198]().\n\n### Language-Specific Handling\nFor CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) languages, the system includes specialized logic to handle character-level spacing:\n*   **Chinese/Japanese**: `clean_cjk_spaces` removes spaces between CJK characters to facilitate CER calculation while preserving spaces for English/Numbers [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py:120-145]().\n*   **Cantonese**: Uses `zhconv` to convert traditional characters to simplified and `cn2an` to transform numbers before character-level splitting [omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py:162-182]().\n\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/eval/wer/text_norm_omni.py:51-111](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/norm_config_module.py:70-108](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py:120-145]()\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Common Utilities (`common.py`)\nThe `common.py` file provides the mathematical foundation for the evaluation scripts.\n*   **`process_one`**: Wraps the `jiwer` library to compute substitutions, deletions, and insertions for a single pair [omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py:27-65]().\n*   **`log_metrics`**: Aggregates error counts across the entire dataset to calculate the final weighted WER percentage [omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py:68-88]().\n\n### Multi-GPU Execution Pattern\nAll evaluation scripts follow a parallel execution pattern using `ProcessPoolExecutor` to distribute ASR inference across available GPUs.\n\n**Code Entity Space: Parallel Evaluation Pattern**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"MainProcess\"\n        M[\"main\"] --\u003e RL[\"read_test_list\"]\n        RL --\u003e PPE[\"ProcessPoolExecutor\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"WorkerProcess\"\n        PPE --\u003e PI[\"process_init\"]\n        PI --\u003e LM[\"load_hubert_model / load_whisper_model\"]\n        LM --\u003e REW[\"run_eval_worker\"]\n        REW --\u003e WP[\"worker_pipe\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"UtilityFunctions\"\n        REW --\u003e PO[\"process_one\"]\n        PO --\u003e PP[\"post_process\"]\n    end\n```\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/eval/wer/hubert.py:199-250](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:183-213](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/common.py:27-48]()\n\n### Backend Specifics\n\n#### HuBERT (LibriSpeech)\nThe HuBERT backend uses `transformers.pipeline` with `hubert-large-ls960-ft`. It specifically targets English WER and applies basic regex-based normalization (lowercase and alphanumeric only) [omnivoice/eval/wer/hubert.py:120-156]().\n\n#### Seed-TTS / MiniMax\nThis backend employs a dual-model approach:\n1.  **Whisper-large-v3**: Used for English transcription [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:106-125]().\n2.  **Paraformer-zh**: Used for Chinese transcription via the `funasr.AutoModel` [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:128-153]().\nThe `post_process` function here handles the splitting of Chinese characters with spaces to ensure `jiwer` treats each character as a \"word\" for CER calculation [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:156-180]().\n\n#### FLEURS (Omnilingual)\nThe FLEURS script uses the `omnilingual_asr` package. It includes a complex language mapping system (`iso_639_3_id_to_mixed_id`) to reconcile differences between OmniVoice language codes and the ASR model's expected identifiers [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py:81-118]().\n\n#### SenseVoice (Cantonese)\nThe `sensevoice.py` script specifically targets Cantonese (`yue`) CER using `iic/SenseVoiceSmall` [omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py:118-124](). It utilizes `zhconv` for script conversion and `cn2an` for number normalization before character-level splitting [omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py:175-180]().\n\n**Sources:** [omnivoice/eval/wer/hubert.py:120-141](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/seedtts.py:128-153](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/fleurs.py:48-53](), [omnivoice/eval/wer/sensevoice.py:116-124]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2d:T2030,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Speaker Similarity (SIM-o) Evaluation\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py](omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py](omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Speaker Similarity (SIM-o) evaluation subsystem provides a quantitative measure of how closely a generated audio sample matches the vocal characteristics of a reference speaker. This is a critical metric for the **Voice Cloning** mode, where the model's ability to preserve speaker identity is paramount.\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe evaluation utilizes a hybrid architecture combining a Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) front-end with a specialized speaker verification back-end.\n\n### ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM Model\nThe core model, defined in `ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM` [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:25-34](), integrates two powerful components:\n\n1.  **WavLM Large Front-end**: A `wavlm_large` model is used as the feature extractor [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:38-46](). It extracts high-level speech representations from raw waveforms. The implementation can load WavLM from `s3prl/s3prl` or a local path [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:39-46]().\n2.  **Weighted Feature Aggregation**: The model learns a `feature_weight` parameter [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:64]() to perform a weighted sum of the hidden states from the WavLM layers [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:133-139]().\n3.  **ECAPA-TDNN Back-end**: The aggregated features pass through a series of Squeeze-and-Excitation Res2Blocks (`SE_Res2Block`) [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:71-100]().\n4.  **Attentive Statistics Pooling**: The `AttentiveStatsPool` layer [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:105-109]() calculates the mean and standard deviation of the frame-level features, weighted by an attention mechanism, to produce a fixed-length speaker embedding.\n\n### Data Flow and Embedding Extraction\nThe model converts raw audio into a 256-dimensional embedding vector [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:129]().\n\n| Stage | Component | Output Shape |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| Input | Raw Waveform (16kHz) | `[Batch, Samples]` |\n| SSL Feature Extraction | `wavlm_large` | `[24, Batch, Frames, 1024]` |\n| Aggregation | `feature_weight` (Softmax) | `[Batch, 1024, Frames]` |\n| TDNN Layers | `SE_Res2Block` (x3) | `[Batch, 512, Frames]` |\n| Pooling | `AttentiveStatsPool` | `[Batch, 1024]` |\n| Projection | `nn.Linear` | `[Batch, 256]` (Embedding) |\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:25-158]()\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:126-132]()\n\n---\n\n## Evaluation Workflow\n\nThe evaluation process is orchestrated by `omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py`. It compares generated audio files against the reference audio specified in a JSONL test list.\n\n### Multi-GPU Parallelization\nTo handle large-scale evaluation, the system uses a `ProcessPoolExecutor` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:29]() to distribute workloads across multiple GPUs.\n\n1.  **Worker Initialization**: The `worker_init` function [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:102-142]() is called for each process. Each worker is assigned a specific GPU rank and initializes its own instance of `ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:126-138]().\n2.  **Task Distribution**: The `main` function reads the test list using `read_test_list` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:207]() and submits tasks to the pool via `executor.submit(run_similarity_worker, ...)` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:225]().\n3.  **Similarity Computation**: Inside `run_similarity_worker` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:151-181](), the worker extracts embeddings for both the reference and generated audio using `get_embedding` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:145-149](), then calculates the **Cosine Similarity** using `torch.nn.functional.cosine_similarity` [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:165-169]().\n\n### SIM-o Logic Diagram\nThis diagram maps the natural language evaluation steps to the specific code entities responsible for them.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Input Preparation\"\n        A[\"JSONL Test List\"] --\u003e B[\"read_test_list()\"]\n        B --\u003e C[\"ProcessPoolExecutor\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Worker Process (GPU-bound)\"\n        C --\u003e D[\"worker_init()\"]\n        D --\u003e E[\"ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM\"]\n        E --\u003e F[\"run_similarity_worker()\"]\n        \n        F --\u003e G[\"load_eval_waveform()\"]\n        G --\u003e H[\"get_embedding()\"]\n        H --\u003e I[\"torch.nn.functional.cosine_similarity\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Results\"\n        I --\u003e J[\"SIM-o Score (0.0 to 1.0)\"]\n        J --\u003e K[\"decode-path (Output File)\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:102-181]()\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:207-225]()\n- [omnivoice/eval/utils.py:36]()\n- [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:37]()\n\n---\n\n## CLI Interface: `sim.py`\n\nThe SIM-o evaluation is executed via the `omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py` entrypoint [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:183]().\n\n### Arguments Reference\n| Flag | Type | Description |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| `--wav-path` | `str` | Directory containing the generated audio files to be evaluated [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:52-56](). |\n| `--test-list` | `str` | Path to the JSONL file containing metadata (must include `id` and `ref_audio`) [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:58-63](). |\n| `--model-dir` | `str` | Directory containing `wavlm_large_finetune.pth` and `wavlm_large/` config [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:65-72](). |\n| `--decode-path`| `str` | (Optional) Path to save detailed per-sample results [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:80-85](). |\n| `--nj-per-gpu` | `int` | Number of parallel processes to spawn per available GPU [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:87-91](). |\n\n### Example Usage\n```bash\npython3 omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py \\\n    --wav-path ./outputs/generated_audio \\\n    --test-list ./data/test_sets/librispeech_test.jsonl \\\n    --model-dir ./models/tts_eval_models \\\n    --decode-path ./results/sim_scores.txt \\\n    --nj-per-gpu 2\n```\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:47-92]()\n- [omnivoice/eval/speaker_similarity/sim.py:196-203]()\n\n---\n\n## Model Components and Layers\n\nThe `ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM` architecture relies on several custom modules to process the SSL features effectively.\n\n### Key Classes\n*   **`SE_Res2Block`**: Implements the Res2Net bottleneck block with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks. It uses dilated convolutions to increase the receptive field [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:71-100]().\n*   **`AttentiveStatsPool`**: Instead of simple global average pooling, this module learns which frames are more important for speaker identity [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:105-109]().\n*   **`Conv1dReluBn`**: A standard utility block for 1D convolution followed by ReLU and Batch Normalization [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:229-231]().\n*   **`Res2Conv1dReluBn`**: A multi-scale residual block used within the architecture to capture features at different granularities [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:167-181]().\n\n### Architecture Diagram (Code Entity Space)\nThe following diagram illustrates the internal class structure of the model defined in `ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py`.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"ECAPA_TDNN_WAVLM\"\n        WAV[\"wavlm_large (S3PRL)\"] --\u003e AGG[\"Weighted Sum (feature_weight)\"]\n        AGG --\u003e LN[\"InstanceNorm1d\"]\n        LN --\u003e L1[\"Conv1dReluBn (Layer 1)\"]\n        L1 --\u003e L2[\"SE_Res2Block (Layer 2)\"]\n        L2 --\u003e L3[\"SE_Res2Block (Layer 3)\"]\n        L3 --\u003e L4[\"SE_Res2Block (Layer 4)\"]\n        \n        L2 \u0026 L3 \u0026 L4 --\u003e CAT[\"torch.cat\"]\n        CAT --\u003e CONV[\"nn.Conv1d (1x1)\"]\n        CONV --\u003e POOL[\"AttentiveStatsPool\"]\n        POOL --\u003e BN[\"nn.BatchNorm1d\"]\n        BN --\u003e LIN[\"nn.Linear (Embedding)\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:**\n- [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:25-158]()\n- [omnivoice/eval/models/ecapa_tdnn_wavlm.py:167-222]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2e:T18ad,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# UTMOS (Naturalness / MOS) Evaluation\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py](omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py)\n- [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py](omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe UTMOS evaluation subsystem provides an automated Mean Opinion Score (MOS) prediction system to assess the naturalness of synthesized speech. It utilizes a \"strong learner\" architecture based on the Saeki 2022 paper, combining Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) features with a Transformer-based encoder and a BiLSTM-projection head to predict human-like naturalness scores.\n\n## UTMOS Model Architecture\n\nThe core architecture is implemented in `UTMOS22Strong`, which acts as a non-intrusive MOS predictor (it does not require a reference ground-truth audio). It maps raw waveforms to a scalar score typically ranging from 1 to 5.\n\n### Key Components\n\n1.  **Wav2Vec2 Feature Extractor**: A `Wav2Vec2Model` [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:96-131]() that processes raw audio into latent representations. It consists of a `ConvFeatureExtractionModel` [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:133-171]() followed by a `TransformerEncoder` [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:173-214]().\n2.  **Domain \u0026 Judge Embeddings**: The model uses non-trainable (fixed) embeddings for `domain_emb` and `judge_emb` [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:52-57](). These are expanded and concatenated with the SSL features to provide context to the recurrent layers [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:77-82]().\n3.  **BiLSTM Head**: A bidirectional LSTM processes the concatenated feature series [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:58-63]().\n4.  **Projection \u0026 Scaling**: A linear projection layer reduces the hidden dimensions to a single value per frame [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:64-66](). The final utterance-level score is calculated by averaging these frame scores and applying a linear transformation: `score * 2 + 3` [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:91]().\n\n### Model Data Flow\n\nThe following diagram traces the transformation of a raw waveform into a MOS score within the `UTMOS22Strong` class.\n\n**UTMOS Score Computation Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"UTMOS22Strong.forward\"\n        WAV[\"wave (B, T)\"] --\u003e W2V[\"Wav2Vec2Model\"]\n        W2V --\u003e FEAT[\"unit_series (B, Frame, 768)\"]\n        \n        DOM[\"domain_emb\"] --\u003e D_EXP[\"domain_series (B, Frame, 128)\"]\n        JUD[\"judge_emb\"] --\u003e J_EXP[\"judge_series (B, Frame, 128)\"]\n        \n        FEAT --\u003e CAT[\"torch.cat\"]\n        D_EXP --\u003e CAT\n        J_EXP --\u003e CAT\n        \n        CAT --\u003e LSTM[\"self.blstm (BiLSTM)\"]\n        LSTM --\u003e PROJ[\"self.projection (Linear/ReLU)\"]\n        PROJ --\u003e S_SERIES[\"score_series (B, Frame, 1)\"]\n        \n        S_SERIES --\u003e MEAN[\"mean(dim=1)\"]\n        MEAN --\u003e SCALE[\"* 2 + 3\"]\n        SCALE --\u003e OUT[\"utter_score (B,)\"]\n    end\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:32-94]()\n\n---\n\n## Evaluation Pipeline\n\nThe evaluation is managed by `omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py`, which provides a multi-GPU CLI for scoring large test sets.\n\n### Initialization and Worker Logic\nThe system uses a `ProcessPoolExecutor` [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:219-224]() to distribute audio files across available GPUs.\n*   **Worker Setup**: Each worker process is initialized via `worker_init` [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:101-134](), which assigns a specific CUDA device via `get_device` [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:94-98]() and loads the `UTMOS22Strong` weights.\n*   **Audio Loading**: Waveforms are loaded at a fixed sampling rate of 16,000 Hz using `load_eval_waveform` [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:143]().\n*   **Inference**: The `run_utmos_worker` function [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:136-157]() performs the forward pass and handles errors for individual files.\n\n### CLI Interface\nThe main entry point parses a JSONL test list to identify the synthesized audio files to be evaluated.\n\n| Argument | Description |\n| :--- | :--- |\n| `--wav-path` | Directory containing the generated `.wav` files [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:51-55](). |\n| `--test-list` | Path to the JSONL file containing metadata (ids, language) [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:57-62](). |\n| `--model-dir` | Path to the directory containing `utmos22_strong_step7459_v1.pt` [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:64-71](). |\n| `--nj-per-gpu` | Number of worker processes to spawn per GPU (default: 1) [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:86-90](). |\n| `--decode-path` | Optional file path to save per-file scores [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:79-84](). |\n\n**Code Entity Mapping: CLI to Model**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"CLI Space (omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py)\"\n        MAIN[\"main()\"] --\u003e READ[\"read_test_list()\"]\n        MAIN --\u003e POOL[\"ProcessPoolExecutor\"]\n        POOL --\u003e W_INIT[\"worker_init()\"]\n        POOL --\u003e W_RUN[\"run_utmos_worker()\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Model Space (omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py)\"\n        W_INIT --\u003e MODEL_CLASS[\"UTMOS22Strong\"]\n        W_RUN --\u003e FORWARD[\"UTMOS22Strong.forward()\"]\n        FORWARD --\u003e W2V_MOD[\"Wav2Vec2Model\"]\n    end\n\n    READ -- \"Provides wav_path\" --\u003e W_RUN\n    MODEL_CLASS -- \"Instantiates\" --\u003e FORWARD\n```\nSources: [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:46-91](), [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:159-224]()\n\n---\n\n## Technical Implementation Details\n\n### Feature Extraction Specifics\nThe `Wav2Vec2Model` uses a standard architecture for speech SSL:\n*   **Conv Layers**: 7 layers of 1D convolutions with specific strides `[5, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2]` to downsample the raw audio [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:103-105]().\n*   **Transformer**: 12 layers of `TransformerSentenceEncoderLayer` with 12 attention heads and an embedding dimension of 768 [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:178-206]().\n*   **Positional Encoding**: Uses a convolutional positional encoding (`pos_conv`) with a kernel size of 128 [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:194-204]().\n\n### Score Aggregation\nAfter the worker processes finish, the main process aggregates scores by language [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:228-245](). It calculates:\n1.  **Mean UTMOS**: The average score across the entire test set.\n2.  **Per-Language UTMOS**: A breakdown of naturalness scores for each language present in the test list metadata.\n\nSources: [omnivoice/eval/models/utmos.py:96-131](), [omnivoice/eval/mos/utmos.py:228-250]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2f:T1dd3,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Glossary\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [README.md](README.md)\n- [docs/OmniVoice.ipynb](docs/OmniVoice.ipynb)\n- [docs/community-projects.md](docs/community-projects.md)\n- [docs/tips.md](docs/tips.md)\n- [docs/voice-design.md](docs/voice-design.md)\n- [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py](omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py)\n- [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py](omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py)\n- [omnivoice/training/builder.py](omnivoice/training/builder.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py](omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/duration.py](omnivoice/utils/duration.py)\n- [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py](omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis glossary defines technical terms, domain-specific concepts, and codebase entities used within the OmniVoice project. It serves as a reference for onboarding engineers to understand the internal mechanics and data structures of the model.\n\n## Core Concepts \u0026 Architecture\n\n### Diffusion Language Model\nOmniVoice utilizes a \"Diffusion Language Model\" architecture. Unlike traditional autoregressive models that predict one token at a time, this model performs iterative unmasking across multiple codebook layers simultaneously. It is built upon a Qwen-based LLM backbone.\n*   **Implementation**: `OmniVoice` class in [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:193-213]().\n*   **Backbone**: Initialized via `AutoModel.from_config(self.config.llm_config)` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:207-207]().\n\n### Audio Tokens (Discrete Representation)\nAudio is represented as a multi-layered grid of discrete tokens. OmniVoice typically uses 8 layers (codebooks) of tokens.\n*   **Vocab Size**: Default is 1025 (1024 values + 1 mask token) [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:162-163]().\n*   **Codebooks**: Defined by `num_audio_codebook` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:165-165]().\n*   **Offsets**: Codebook layers are flattened into a single sequence using `codebook_layer_offsets` to distinguish tokens from different layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:213-216]().\n\n### Iterative Unmasking\nThe process of generating audio by starting with a sequence of mask tokens (`audio_mask_id`) and gradually replacing them with predicted audio tokens over several steps.\n*   **Step Control**: Regulated by `num_step` (default 32) in `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:93-93]().\n*   **Penalty**: `layer_penalty_factor` encourages lower (more significant) codebook layers to be unmasked before higher layers [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:96-96]().\n\n### Classifier-Free Guidance (CFG)\nA technique used during inference to improve the alignment between the conditioning (text/voice prompt) and the generated output by combining conditional and unconditional logit predictions.\n*   **Parameter**: `guidance_scale` in `OmniVoiceGenerationConfig` [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:94-94]().\n\n**Sources**: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:92-103](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:157-183](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:193-216]().\n\n---\n\n## Voice Generation Modes\n\nOmniVoice supports three primary modes of operation, all routed through the `model.generate()` method [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:23-24]().\n\n| Mode | Description | Key Code Entities |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Voice Cloning** | Uses a short reference audio to mimic a specific speaker. | `VoiceClonePrompt`, `ref_audio`, `ref_text` |\n| **Voice Design** | Generates a voice based on text descriptions (e.g., \"male, low pitch\"). | `instruct`, `_INSTRUCT_CATEGORIES` |\n| **Auto Voice** | The model selects a voice automatically without specific conditioning. | `text` (only) |\n\n### System Mapping: Generation Space to Code Space\n\nThe following diagram illustrates how high-level generation concepts map to specific classes and functions in the codebase.\n\n**Generation Mode Mapping**\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph \"Natural Language Space\"\n        A[\"'Clone this voice'\"]\n        B[\"'Design a male voice'\"]\n        C[\"'Just speak this'\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Code Entity Space\"\n        D[\"OmniVoice.generate()\"]\n        E[\"VoiceClonePrompt\"]\n        F[\"_INSTRUCT_CATEGORIES\"]\n        G[\"RuleDurationEstimator\"]\n        H[\"GenerationTask\"]\n    end\n\n    A --\u003e|\"ref_audio/ref_text\"| D\n    D --\u003e E\n    B --\u003e|\"instruct='male'\"| D\n    D --\u003e F\n    C --\u003e|\"text='...'\"| D\n    D --\u003e H\n    H -.-\u003e|\"Estimates Length\"| G\n```\n**Sources**: [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:84-103](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:113-143](), [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:31-66](), [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:31-46]().\n\n---\n\n## Data \u0026 Training Terms\n\n### WebDataset (.tar + .jsonl)\nThe training format uses Sharded Tar files for high-performance I/O.\n*   **Reader**: Handled by `WebDatasetReader` which parses manifests to stream shards [omnivoice/training/builder.py:147-147]().\n*   **Sample Processor**: `OmniVoiceSampleProcessor` handles tokenization, masking, and prompt injection during training [omnivoice/training/builder.py:131-142]().\n\n### Duration Estimation\nWhen a user does not provide a specific `duration` or `speed` during inference, the model must estimate how long the resulting audio should be based on the input text.\n*   **Logic**: The `RuleDurationEstimator` assigns phonetic weights to different Unicode scripts (e.g., CJK characters are weighted 3.0, Latin 1.0) [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:39-66]().\n*   **Usage**: The `RuleDurationEstimator` is initialized within `OmniVoice.from_pretrained` and used to compute `target_lens` [omnivoice/cli/infer_batch.py:207-222]().\n\n### Voice Design Attributes\nA set of mutually exclusive categories used to \"design\" a voice.\n*   **Categories**: Gender, Age, Pitch, Style (Whisper), Accent (English), and Dialect (Chinese) [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:31-46]().\n*   **Normalization**: The system normalizes these attributes between English and Chinese (e.g., \"male\" $\\leftrightarrow$ \"男\") [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:48-55]().\n\n---\n\n## Code Structure \u0026 Data Flow\n\nThe diagram below shows the data flow from raw inputs to a trained model state via the training builder.\n\n**Training Pipeline Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Input Config\"\n        TC[\"TrainingConfig\"]\n        DC[\"Data Config JSON\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Data Loading (training/builder.py)\"\n        WDR[\"WebDatasetReader\"]\n        OSP[\"OmniVoiceSampleProcessor\"]\n        PID[\"PackingIterableDataset\"]\n        PDC[\"PackingDataCollator\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph \"Model Initialization\"\n        OV[\"OmniVoice\"]\n        TOK[\"AutoTokenizer\"]\n    end\n\n    TC --\u003e|\"build_model_and_tokenizer\"| OV\n    TC --\u003e|\"build_model_and_tokenizer\"| TOK\n    DC --\u003e WDR\n    WDR --\u003e PID\n    TOK --\u003e OSP\n    OSP --\u003e PID\n    PID --\u003e PDC\n    PDC --\u003e|\"train_loader\"| TRAIN[\"OmniTrainer\"]\n```\n**Sources**: [omnivoice/training/builder.py:49-122](), [omnivoice/training/builder.py:125-182](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:25-26]().\n\n---\n\n## Evaluation Metrics\n\nOmniVoice uses three primary metrics to determine model quality:\n\n1.  **WER/CER (Word/Character Error Rate)**: Measures intelligibility using ASR models. The `language_name` field in test lists is used for reporting these results [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:37-39]().\n2.  **SIM-o (Speaker Similarity)**: Measures how well a cloned voice matches the reference.\n3.  **UTMOS**: A neural-based Mean Opinion Score (MOS) predictor that evaluates the naturalness of the generated audio.\n\n**Sources**: [README.md:113-176](), [omnivoice/models/omnivoice.py:84-150](), [omnivoice/utils/duration.py:31-158](), [omnivoice/utils/voice_design.py:27-66](), [omnivoice/utils/data_utils.py:29-68]()."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"5:[\"$\",\"$L15\",null,{\"repoName\":\"k2-fsa/OmniVoice\",\"hasConfig\":false,\"canSteer\":true,\"children\":[\"$\",\"$L16\",null,{\"wiki\":{\"metadata\":{\"repo_name\":\"k2-fsa/OmniVoice\",\"commit_hash\":\"4a4b2295\",\"generated_at\":\"2026-04-21T14:58:45.878994\",\"config\":null,\"config_source\":\"none\"},\"pages\":[{\"page_plan\":{\"id\":\"1\",\"title\":\"OmniVoice
